error.component_not_ready:
{command} failed: {component} is not initialized.
Please run /lmem status to check plugin status; if abnormal, check startup logs.

status: Confirm memory engine initialization is complete
Check logs for exception stack trace to locate specific module
search: Confirm keyword is not empty and of reasonable length
Confirm database and index files exist and are readable/writable
Check logs for retrieval component initialization failure
forget: Confirm memory ID exists and belongs to currently accessible data
Confirm database is not occupied by another process for a long time
Check logs for delete call stack
rebuild_index: Confirm database and index files are readable/writable
Locate failed documents from logs and retry rebuild
rebuild_graph: Confirm graph memory feature is enabled
Confirm database and index files are readable/writable
Check logs to locate specific failed documents
summarize: Confirm current session has at least 2 unsummarized messages
Check logs for summary processing stack trace
reset: Confirm session ID is valid and session storage is accessible
Check logs for clear_session call stack
cleanup: Confirm current session has readable historical records
Check logs for cleanup call stack

search.query_empty: Query keyword cannot be empty. Example: /lmem search project progress 5
search.no_results: No memories related to '{query}' found. Try shorter keywords or increase parameter k.

forget.id_invalid: Memory ID must be a non-negative integer. Example: /lmem forget 123
forget.not_found:
Deletion failed: memory #{id} does not exist.
Please confirm the memory ID using /lmem search or WebUI.

rebuild_index.partial_notice: Note: This rebuild had a few failures, failure rate {ratio:.2%}, within safe threshold. You can run /lmem rebuild-index again later to complete.

rebuild_graph.starting: Starting graph memory index rebuild, this may take some time...
rebuild_graph.success:
Graph memory rebuild complete.

• Rebuilt: {rebuilt} items
• Skipped: {skipped} items

webui.guide:
AMA-10 Memory Management Interface

Please access via AstrBot official WebUI:
• Open AstrBot Dashboard
• Go to Plugins -> AMA-10 Memory -> Pages -> dashboard

Tips:
• Plugin page reuses AstrBot login state, no extra login needed
• Requires AstrBot >= 4.24.2

Available in interface:
• Memory editing and management
• Visual statistics and analysis
• Graph browsing and recall debugging
• Data migration and index maintenance

summarize.starting:
Starting manual memory summarization...
Message range: [{start}:{end}], total {count} items
summarize.fetch_failed:
Failed to fetch messages: no summarizable messages read.
Please confirm the current session has historical messages and retry.

reset.success:
Long-term memory context for current session has been reset.

Next memory summarization will start from now and will not include previous conversation content.

cleanup.starting: {mode_text}Starting cleanup of memory injection fragments in AstrBot historical messages...
cleanup.context_unavailable:
Cleanup failed: Unable to access AstrBot Context.
Please confirm the plugin is running in a full AstrBot context and retry.
cleanup.no_history: Current session has no conversation history, no cleanup needed.
cleanup.empty_history: Current conversation history is empty, no cleanup needed.
cleanup.parse_failed:
Failed to parse conversation history: data is not valid JSON.
Please check if session storage content was modified by external tools.
cleanup.result_template:
{mode_text}Cleanup complete.

Statistics:
• Scanned messages: {scanned} items
• Matched memory fragments: {matched} items
• Cleaned content: {cleaned} items
• Deleted messages: {deleted} items

{notice}
cleanup.notice_preview: This is preview mode, no actual data was modified. Use /lmem cleanup exec to perform actual cleanup.
cleanup.notice_exec: AstrBot conversation history has been updated, memory injection fragments have been cleaned.

help.text:
AMA-10 Memory User Guide

Core commands:
/lmem status              View system status
/lmem search <keyword> [count]  Search memories (default 5)
/lmem forget <ID>          Delete specified memory
/lmem rebuild-index       Rebuild index (fix inconsistency)
/lmem rebuild-graph       Rebuild graph memory index (backfill old data)
/lmem webui               Show WebUI management interface entry
/lmem summarize           Immediately trigger memory summarization for current session
/lmem reset               Reset current session memory context
/lmem cleanup [preview|exec] Clean up memory injection fragments in historical messages (default preview)
/lmem help                Show this help

Usage tips:
• Use search command for daily queries
• For complex management, go to Plugins -> AMA-10 Memory -> Pages -> dashboard in AstrBot Dashboard
• Memories are automatically saved from conversations
• Use forget to delete sensitive information
• Execute rebuild-index when index is inconsistent
• After enabling graph memory, execute rebuild-graph to backfill old data
• After plugin update, execute cleanup to clean old data

cleanup command examples:
  /lmem cleanup          # Preview mode, shows statistics only
  /lmem cleanup preview  # Same as above
  /lmem cleanup exec     # Perform actual cleanup

More info: https://github.com/preca-hoshino/astrbot_plugin_ama-10_memory

init.ready: Plugin is ready. Use /lmem help to view available commands.
init.failed:
Plugin initialization failed.
Error details: {error}

Please check:
1. Whether Embedding Provider is correctly configured and callable
2. Whether LLM Provider is available
3. Whether plugin data directory has read/write permissions
4. Exception stack trace in startup logs
init.in_progress:
Plugin is initializing in the background.
Provider check attempts so far: {attempts}

If it takes too long, please check:
1. Embedding Provider and LLM Provider configuration
2. Whether other plugins are blocking initialization
3. Whether Provider-related errors appear in logs

command.not_ready:
Command handler is not ready, current command cannot be executed.
Please run /lmem status to check plugin status; if it keeps failing, check initialization logs.
command.core_not_ready:
Plugin core components are not initialized.
Please run /lmem status to check initialization status; if it still fails, check the exception stack trace in startup logs.
